challenges.better_path: Treat release_readiness as the go/no-go surface; keep user-visible claims evidence-scoped until it clears.
challenges.current_path: Use green tests, rendered graph, or generated reports as a release proxy.
challenges.first_principles_test: A decision system is release-ready only when evidence gates, multi-topic regression, and high-confidence Claim Card gates are closed.
challenges.risk: Graph/demo progress can be mistaken for scientific readiness.

challenges.better_path: Keep broad crawling alive, but route engineering attention to topic-gap-repair and stale-contract/unattempted-PDF queues before promoting Dossier/Radar output.
challenges.current_path: Continue broad PDF/section crawling as a background substrate.
challenges.first_principles_test: Evidence acquisition should maximize decision lift per parsed paper, especially for benchmark-topic turning papers, future endpoints, and Claim Card inputs.
challenges.risk: Full-corpus crawling can consume time while the benchmark-topic decision gaps remain open.

challenges.better_path: At the first safe section-ingest boundary, run post-frontfill-chain so section-embeddings, chains, Step5c/6/13, and audits rebuild together.
challenges.current_path: Use atom exact/fuzzy search now; wait to materialize section-level fuzzy context.
challenges.first_principles_test: Fuzzy retrieval can widen recall, but every retrieved context must remain candidate-only and must be available before downstream Claim Cards rely on it.
challenges.risk: Long-section semantic recall is code-complete but absent from the live DB; Step13 may miss context until post-frontfill rebuild.

challenges.better_path: Keep main-path output as low-linked-ref context and prioritize exact cited-work backfill plus relinking.
challenges.current_path: Use current citation/main-path graph while linked references remain sparse.
challenges.first_principles_test: Citation evolution and main-path claims require enough linked references to distinguish field history from local-corpus sampling artifacts.
challenges.risk: Main path can look causal while actually reflecting missing cited works.

challenges.better_path: Keep raw future edges in candidate_pool and focus repair on complete five-question Claim Cards with falsifiable experiments.
challenges.current_path: Use GNN/VGAE future candidates as growth signal.
challenges.first_principles_test: A future edge is only an inspection target until calibrated evidence, Step6 fusion, and a complete/high-confidence Step13 Claim Card exist.
challenges.risk: Candidate ranking can be misread as investable direction confidence.

challenges.better_path: Continue conservative OpenAlex/local field-topic repair and label cross-field claims with uncertainty until coverage improves.
challenges.current_path: Use OpenAlex/local field-topic enrichment as context.
challenges.first_principles_test: Field/topic context is useful only as uncertainty-aware context; it cannot substitute for local section evidence or linked citation evidence.
challenges.risk: Cross-field claims may look broader than the current metadata support allows.

policy: This audit challenges the current route before more execution. It cannot promote claims; it only redirects effort toward the evidence path most likely to produce auditable Topic Dossiers, Evolution Evidence Maps, and Claim Cards.
